name: Drupal Ecosystem
description: Drupal is more than a CMS — it is a framework, a global community of initiatives and local organizations, and an ecosystem of companies that build products and services around it.

children.name: Code
children.description: Drupal Core, contributed modules, and the tools developers use to build and run Drupal sites.

children.name: Drupal Core
children.description: The core software: PHP framework, content modeling, rendering, configuration, and the foundational modules every Drupal site uses.

children.name: Contributed modules
children.description: Tens of thousands of community-maintained modules and themes that extend Drupal — from Views and Paragraphs to commerce, search, and AI integrations.

children.name: Tools
children.description: Command-line and local-development tools the Drupal community builds and shares.

children.name: Drush
children.description: The Drupal Shell — the standard command-line tool for administering Drupal sites: cache clears, config sync, user management, scripting.

children.name: DDEV
children.description: A Docker-based local development environment widely used by Drupal teams; one of several local-dev tools the community relies on.

children.name: Community
children.description: The people, initiatives, and organizations behind Drupal — globally and regionally.

children.name: Initiatives
children.description: Project-wide efforts coordinated by community members and the Drupal Association.

children.name: Composer Support in Core
children.description: Initiative to make Composer the supported way to install and update Drupal Core.

children.name: Drupal AI
children.description: A growing set of contributed modules and an associated initiative bringing AI capabilities into Drupal site building and editorial workflows.

children.name: Innovation eXchange Program (IXP)
children.description: Community program coordinating innovation work and contributions across the project.

children.name: Community Working Group
children.description: Volunteer group that supports the health of the Drupal community and upholds its code of conduct.

children.name: Organizations
children.description: Regional and national associations that organize the community on the ground.

children.name: United States
children.description: US-based Drupal community organizations and their events.

children.name: Drupal Association
children.description: The non-profit that supports the Drupal project worldwide, runs drupal.org, and organizes the flagship DrupalCon conferences.

children.name: DrupalCon North America
children.description: Annual flagship North American conference organized by the Drupal Association.

children.name: Florida Drupal Community
children.description: Regional Drupal community in Florida; organizes Florida DrupalCamp annually.

children.name: Florida DrupalCamp
children.description: Annual regional DrupalCamp in Florida.

children.name: Texas Drupal Community
children.description: Regional Drupal community in Texas; organizes Texas DrupalCamp annually.

children.name: Texas DrupalCamp
children.description: Annual regional DrupalCamp in Texas.

children.name: Europe
children.description: European Drupal community organizations and their events.

children.name: European Drupal Association
children.description: Coordinates the European DrupalCon and supports regional camps across Europe.

children.name: DrupalCon Europe
children.description: Annual flagship European conference.

children.name: Latin America
children.description: Drupal community organizations and camps across Latin America.

children.name: Regional camps (TBD)
children.description: Concrete camps and communities to be named here as we add them.

children.name: Private Organizations
children.description: Companies that build products, sell services, sponsor events, and employ Drupal contributors.

children.name: United States
children.description: US-based Drupal companies.

children.name: Acquia
children.description: The largest commercial Drupal-focused vendor; provides hosting, digital experience products, and is a long-time Drupal Association sponsor.

children.name: Pantheon
children.description: Web ops platform for Drupal (and WordPress); hosting, workflow tooling, and CDN.

children.name: Palantir.net
children.description: Chicago-based Drupal agency serving higher-ed, healthcare, and large publishers.

children.name: Four Kitchens
children.description: Distributed Drupal agency working on large-scale publishing and government sites.

children.name: Tag1 Consulting
children.description: Engineering-heavy consultancy specializing in Drupal performance, scaling, and migrations.

children.name: Kanopi Studios
children.description: Design and Drupal/WordPress agency focused on mission-driven organizations.

children.name: Latin America
children.description: Latin American Drupal companies.

children.name: Seed
children.description: Colombia-based Drupal team.

children.name: ParallelDev
children.description: Costa Rica-based Drupal team.

children.name: Bluefly
children.description: A platform sustaining independent developers and small companies who build community, tools, and products across the Drupal ecosystem.

children.name: Independent Developers
children.description: Individual contributors and freelancers whose work Bluefly supports.

children.name: Examples (TBD)
children.description: Independent developers to be named here with their consent.

children.name: Small Companies
children.description: Small Drupal-focused companies whose work Bluefly supports.

children.name: Examples (TBD)
children.description: Small companies to be named here with their consent.
